title: Lagrangian Hofer-Zehnder Capacities and Energy-Capacity Inequalities
speaker: Sam Lisi
abstract: Rieser and I introduced a variation on the Hofer-Zehnder capacity, relative to a coisotropic submanifold. Revisiting this construction in the case in which the coisotropic submanifold is Lagrangian, we use a persistent homology approach to filtered Floer theory (following Polterovich and Shelukhin) to obtain two energy-capacity inequalities, a local one and a global one. We also show that one of these breaks down if we don't impose topological conditions on the admissible Hamiltonians. This suggests that our capacity is closely related to Barraud-Cornea's real Gromov radius. This is joint work with Antonio Rieser.

proof_stages.label: Lagrangian capacity framework for pairs
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up a numerical invariant c(W,L) of a symplectic manifold together with a properly embedded Lagrangian, requiring monotonicity under embeddings of pairs, scaling like area, and a nontriviality/normalization condition.

proof_stages.label: Hofer-Zehnder-style Lagrangian capacities
proof_stages.summary: Two capacities c(W,L) and c-hat(W,L) are defined by Hofer-Zehnder-type admissible autonomous Hamiltonians, replacing short periodic orbits by short Lagrangian chords and adding for c-hat the requirement that the maximum set on L be a ball.

proof_stages.label: Energy-capacity inequalities and displacement energy
proof_stages.summary: The main results assert a global inequality bounding c-hat by the displacement energy of a compact monotone Lagrangian, and a local inequality bounding c(U,U∩L) by the energy needed to move U off L below a Floer-theoretic threshold δ, with displacement energy measured by Hofer norm.

proof_stages.label: Necessity of monotonicity and the hatted refinement
proof_stages.summary: Counterexamples and model situations show that nonmonotone Lagrangians can have infinite capacity despite being displaceable, and that the unhatted capacity can miss the displacement direction unless the maximum set is constrained as in c-hat.

proof_stages.label: Cartoon Floer/pearl proof of the global inequality
proof_stages.summary: The heuristic proof uses Lagrangian quantum/pearl homology: displaceability forces the fundamental class represented near a maximum to be killed by a positive-action disk, and the ball-shaped maximum set forces the corresponding Floer generator to have low Hamiltonian value, giving the capacity bound.

proof_stages.label: Filtered Lagrangian Floer complex and no-crossing continuation
proof_stages.summary: For nondegenerate Hamiltonians on a monotone Lagrangian, capped chords generate an action-filtered Floer complex over a Novikov variable, and continuation maps can be made action-preserving when no generator crosses the relevant action level.

proof_stages.label: Degenerate Hamiltonians, local Floer homology, and persistence
proof_stages.summary: Filtered homology for degenerate admissible Hamiltonians is defined by small perturbations, the local Floer homology of the c-hat maximum is identified with Z/2 in degree n, and exact triangles/R^2 persistence track the action-spectrum crossings to rigorously obtain the final displacement-energy bound.
